headline: How To Build A $7,500 Monthly Paycheck Without Flipping A Single House
summary: For many investors, real estate seems like the default path to passive income. Buy a rental, collect the checks, repeat. In reality, many landlords discover they have purchased a second job, complete with late-night phone calls, surprise repairs, insurance headaches, and months when the rent simply doesn’t arrive. headline: Verizon (VZ) Launches Simplicity And Verizon One Plans To Win Back Subscribers
summary: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) has introduced two new wireless offerings, the Simplicity plan and the Verizon One plan, in response to recent subscriber losses. The Simplicity plan focuses on customizable, affordable wireless bundles aimed at consumers seeking clearer pricing and flexibility. Verizon One combines mobile and home internet services in a single package for new customers, targeting households that want integrated connectivity. These moves mark a shift in Verizon’s consumer...

headline: You Don’t Need a Powerball Jackpot To Build A $5,000 Monthly Dividend Paycheck
summary: The latest Powerball jackpot stood at roughly $269 million, with a cash option of about $120.5 million before taxes. That would solve all your financial problems, but the odds are not great: roughly 1 in 292 million. If your goal is simply to create a $5,000 monthly dividend paycheck, however, a Powerball win is the ... You Don’t Need a Powerball Jackpot To Build A $5,000 Monthly Dividend Paycheck

headline: Yahoo CEO on Launching an Adtech Agent Network and AI Search
summary: Yahoo chief executive Jim Lanzone discusses the company's latest transformation since spinning out from Verizon in 2021. Lanzone details the launch of Yahoo Scout, a new artificial-intelligence-driven search engine trained entirely on Yahoo's proprietary data.

headline: This Is the Dividend Portfolio That Pays Off Your Kid’s Student Loans
summary: The average federal student loan borrower leaves school owing roughly $38,000, though balances of $50,000 or more are common among graduate students and many private-college graduates. Depending on interest rates and repayment terms, that debt can create a substantial monthly obligation just as a young adult is trying to rent an apartment, buy a first ...
